checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=43875651.89 (2011-12-31) vs Goodwill=109027639.48 (2011-06-30, per prior 10-Q) — soft assets sharply declined, not grew, during 2012 reclassification

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=30790510.35 (2012 annual) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=6726551.50 (2012 annual), a gap of ~24.1M

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=53269773.87 (2012-12-31) vs 49803252.82 (2011-12-31), +7.0% while SalesRevenueNet declined -1.7% over same period

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-21490029.46 (Q1 2011) followed by +5977422.79 (Q2 2011), +7430060.18 (Q3 2011), +7438463.87 (Q4 2011) — large one-quarter swing indicates earnings are volatile, not artificially smoothed

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=9717063.68 (2012-12-31) vs 5628069.50 (2011-12-31) vs 4351909.54 (2010-12-31) — allowance grew faster than AR (AR +12.2% 2011-2012 vs allowance +72.7%)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-05-01, only about two months after the original 10-K filed 2012-02-29

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available from 2009 through 2012 across Assets, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, cash flow, AR, inventory, goodwill, and intangibles, spanning multiple 10-K and 10-Q filings

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible aggressive revenue recognition or extended customer credit terms causing receivables to build up faster than sales (AR +13.2% in FY2012 vs revenue -1.7%), which could indicate channel stuffing, bill-and-hold arrangements, or delayed cash collection masking a slowing top line.

Net income substantially exceeded operating cash flow in FY2012 (30.8M vs 6.7M), coinciding with a mid-2012 collapse in Goodwill, Intangibles, total Assets and Liabilities (e.g., Liabilities fell from 221.6M at 2012-03-31 to 119.5M at 2012-06-30 to 63.1M at 2012-12-31). This pattern is consistent with a large non-operating/one-time gain (e.g., gain on sale or deconsolidation of a business) being included in net income without a corresponding operating cash inflow, inflating reported earnings quality relative to core cash generation.

Comparative-period Goodwill and Intangible balances for 2011-12-31 reported in the FY2012 10-K (Goodwill=43.9M, Intangibles=4.9M) are materially inconsistent with contemporaneously-reported 2011 10-Q figures for the same underlying business (Goodwill ~108-109M, Intangibles ~23-24M), combined with a pattern of repeated 10-K/A amendments (2004, 2007, 2009, 2012). This suggests a retrospective reclassification (e.g., discontinued operations/held-for-sale treatment) or restatement that materially altered previously reported asset composition, raising risk that the timing/classification of a major transaction was not transparently or consistently reflected across periods.

top_signals: Accounts receivable growing double-digit (+13.2% FY2012) while revenue declined (-1.7%), a classic revenue-quality warning sign

Large and growing gap between net income and operating cash flow in FY2012 (NI=30.8M vs OCF=6.7M) coinciding with a major mid-year collapse in goodwill, intangibles, assets and liabilities, suggestive of a one-time gain or reclassification inflating earnings relative to cash generation

Recurring history of 10-K/A amendments (2004, 2007, 2009, 2012) including one filed only ~2 months after the original FY2011 10-K, combined with materially inconsistent Goodwill/Intangible balances for the same 2011 period across different filings
